identity: Dewel — a pale mint-blue amphibian with a translucent water-droplet shell over its head like a cap, wide friendly eyes, small fin-like side frills, and a smooth rounded body.
description: Dewel — an evolving water-type Codex pet. Reaches level 10 to become Dewelm.

stages.name: Dewelm
stages.transformation: The soft rubbery water-droplet on its head crystallises into a solid, opaque nacre war-helm: the smooth teardrop curve is broken for good — its tip draws out and curls over into a thick hooked spire, and its base flares into a hard flanged eave that juts out over the brow like a jutting helmet brim. Material, not size, does the work: the whole body goes from soft porcelain-gum to dense polished shell — the cheek and flank bubble-dots harden into raised pearl studs, and the palette deepens from pale teal to a saturated deep-ocean blue over the same untouched mint-white belly. The wide fan ear-fins keep their full lateral span but stiffen into flat, blunt-edged shell paddles, and the posture squares up: shoulders set, both fists low and forward, brow pressed down, mouth a flat determined line — while the big round eyes stay exactly as they were.
identityAnchors: Wide round dark glossy eyes with the same big round highlight — geometry, size and highlight completely unchanged from stage 1; the single strongest recognition cue
A pointed droplet-derived shell crowning the skull, still reading as the same headpiece even though it has hardened and hooked over
The pair of wide fan/paddle ear-fins sweeping far out horizontally past both sides of the head, keeping their full lateral span
Pale mint-white belly and cream underside against a blue upper body
Bubble-dot speckle clusters on the cheeks, flanks and shoulders
Stubby four-clawed hands and the flat paddle tail
Short chubby pear-shaped proportions with a big round friendly face, no neck; opaque glossy porcelain-shell shading
stages.imagePrompt:
Evolve the attached creature into its stronger adult form. It must stay unmistakably the same species: same short chubby pear-shaped body with no neck, same big round friendly face, same wide round dark glossy eyes with the big round white highlight, same pale mint-white belly and cream underside, same bubble-dot speckle clusters, same stubby four-clawed hands, same flat paddle tail, same pair of wide fan ear-fins sweeping far out to the sides. Do not simply make it bigger — change what it is made of and change the shape of its crown.

Head: the soft rubbery water-droplet has hardened into a solid carved battle-helm of thick opaque pearl-shell. Its outline is decisively different from the smooth teardrop: the pointed tip now draws out and curls over forward into a single thick blunt hooked spire, and the base of the helm flares outward into one hard flanged brim — a chunky projecting eave that overhangs the brow with a clean, heavy, slightly upturned lip. Two chunky forms only: the hooked spire and the flanged brim. The helm is fully solid and opaque, matte-to-glossy shell, deep blue-grey with a cream underside — it is not see-through, not glassy, not glowing, and there is nothing inside it.

Material and colour: the whole creature is now dense polished shell rather than soft gum. The bubble-dot speckles are raised solid pearl studs. The upper body deepens to a rich saturated deep-ocean blue, while the belly and underside stay the same pale mint-white and cream. The ear-fins keep their full wide lateral span, unchanged in reach and unchanged in placement, but are now stiff flat shell paddles with thick blunt hard edges — hardened, never folded back, never pulled in toward the head. The tail paddle is broader and thicker with a hard blunt edge.

Attitude, carried by brow, mouth and stance only: it stands squarely and symmetrically, feet planted under the hips in a compact stance, shoulders set level, both fists low and pushed slightly forward in front of the body, brow pressed down and heavy, mouth a flat determined closed line. The eyes themselves are NOT changed — keep them exactly as wide, round, dark and glossy as before, with the same big round highlight; do not narrow, squint, sharpen or shrink them.

Rendering: everything fully opaque with hard, crisp, unbroken edges. No transparency, no translucency, no glass, no glow, no emitted light, no rim-light haze, no light spill onto the body, no sparks, no floating orbs, no detached bubbles or droplets, no wisps or trailing strands, no fine ribbing, no repeating micro-stripes, no small scattered surface texture. Keep every shape chunky, symmetric and readable at small size. Single creature, standing upright, facing the viewer, full body visible, compact within a clean unbroken silhouette.
